title: Wood Ear Wonde
description: A festive Irish dish featuring wood ear mushrooms, slow-cooked to a flaky texture and seasoned with herbs for a low-sodium option.

ingredients.name: Wood Ear Mushrooms
ingredients.notes: Ensure they are fully hydrated and ready to use.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Use a mild onion variety for less pungency.

ingredients.name: Garlic Cloves
ingredients.notes: Choose soft garlic to prevent crushing.

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Use mild oil for a milder taste.

ingredients.name: Salt and Pepper
ingredients.notes: Used sparingly to keep it low-sodium.

instructions: Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add chopped onion and minced garlic; sauté for about 5 minutes until translucent.
Fold in wood ear mushrooms and thyme sprigs. Season with salt, pepper, and bay leaves if using.
Simmer gently uncovered on low heat for about 1 hour or until the mushrooms are soft but not mushy.
Serve hot as a side dish or over rice.
Optional: Garnish with fresh herbs before serving.
